data.keyword: Where can I find individual therapy in Louisville Kentucky?
data.tag: lmhg ai mode Where can I find individual therapy in Louisville Kentucky?

result.keyword: Where can I find individual therapy in Louisville Kentucky?

items.markdown:
Finding individual therapy in Louisville, KY, is accessible through local counseling centers, telehealth networks, and professional directories. Depending on your insurance coverage, location, and specific mental health needs, you can easily connect with a licensed professional who fits your requirements.[[1]](https://kentuckycounselingcenter.com/kentucky-counseling-center/louisville/)[[2]](https://www.goodtherapy.org/therapists/ky/louisville)[[3]](https://louisvillerecoverycenter.com/mental-health/)[[4]](https://www.betterhelp.com/)[[5]](https://www.hopequre.com/service)

Leading resources and local options for individual therapy include:

1. Local Therapy Centers in Louisville

![](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/07060028-9766-0139-0000-73303f4ea6a6:8)
Evolved Counseling 4.6 (67)  
Mental health service  
Closed 1122 S Shelby St  
Located on S Shelby St, this center offers highly detailed, individualized mental health services and therapy.[[1]](https://www.google.com/searchviewer/10?svid=CAwSHRIbCgNwdnESFENnMHZaeTh4TVhJeE9UZzROSE5rGAo)[[2]](https://evolvecounselingnoco.com/teletherapy/)[[3]](https://www.google.com/searchviewer/10?svid=CAwSHRIbCgNwdnESFENnMHZaeTh4TVhJeE9UZzROSE5rGAo)

![](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/07060028-9766-0139-0000-73303f4ea6a6:9)
Louisville Center for Therapy and Personal Sustainability (LCTPS)5.0 (20)  
Mental health service  
Open 1974 Douglass Blvd  
Located on Douglass Blvd, they provide a range of therapeutic and wellness services tailored to the community.[[1]](https://www.google.com/searchviewer/10?svid=CAwSHRIbCgNwdnESFENnMHZaeTh4TVdvemFEbDROekI1GAo)[[2]](https://www.instagram.com/louisvillecenterfortherapy/)[[3]](https://www.lctps.org/music-therapy-internship)[[4]](https://www.google.com/searchviewer/10?svid=CAwSHRIbCgNwdnESFENnMHZaeTh4TVdvemFEbDROekI1GAo)[[5]](https://kentuckycounselingcenter.com/kentucky-counseling-center/louisville/)

2. Search Directories to Filter by Insurance/Specialty

- **Psychology Today Therapist Finder:** This is the most popular tool to find a therapist in the Louisville area. You can filter practitioners by ZIP code, your specific health insurance, the issues you want to address (e.g., anxiety, trauma), and the type of therapy you need. You can browse available local professionals using the [Psychology Today Louisville Directory](https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/therapists/ky/louisville).[[1]](https://www.succeedsocially.com/findtherapistforteen)[[2]](https://www.tivoligardenscounseling.com/blog/how-to-choose-a-therapist)[[3]](https://www.everydayhealth.com/emotional-health/how-to-find-a-therapist-whos-right-for-you/)[[4]](https://locationindependenttherapists.com/find-a-therapist/)[[5]](https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/treatment-rehab/ky/louisville)
- **Mental Health America of Kentucky:** This local chapter can help point you toward community mental health centers, low-cost or sliding-scale options, and regional support groups. You can explore their resources directly at [Mental Health America of KY](https://mhaky.org/).[[1]](https://hollymaddy.com/links)[[2]](https://www.goodrx.com/health-topic/mental-health/therapy-without-insurance?srsltid=AfmBOoqfv-vuOZV8d4rxnaasTOrHoivlwOzqyMgekGbJOlu3Yhq865YU)[[3]](https://www.betterhelp.com/mental-health/disorders-conditions/psychotherapy/)[[4]](https://www.hamiltoncenter.org/educational/post-traumatic-stress-disorder/)

3. Telehealth Options

If you prefer remote sessions or want access to a wider variety of specialized therapists, platforms like [BetterHelp](https://www.betterhelp.com/) and [Talkspace](https://www.talkspace.com/) connect you with licensed professionals entirely online.[[1]](https://www.choosingtherapy.com/what-kind-of-therapy-do-i-need/)

4. Direct Insurance Portals

Your first step should always be to check your health insurance provider’s website (e.g., Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield, Humana, Aetna) to find an in-network therapist. Using your insurer's official directory ensures that you are connected with a mental health provider whose services are covered by your plan.[[1]](https://www.betterhelp.com/advice/therapy/accessing-therapy-providers/)[[2]](https://mydenvertherapy.com/therapists-in-denver-who-accept-insurance/)[[3]](https://panahicounseling.com/blogs/how-to-find-a-therapist-8-simple-steps-to-make-your-search-process-easier/)[[4]](https://www.scbh.com/therapies/individual-therapy/)

If you tell me **what specific issues you want to address** and **who your health insurance provider is** , I can help narrow down the best local clinics or online platforms for you.
